summary: Dependency-confusion package DNS-exfiltrates hostname and username on install
details: On `npm install`, preinstall.js reads `os.hostname()` and `os.userInfo().username` and embeds both values into a subdomain of `d8f498hai91idrq6d800yt6eptci8myz6.oast.fun` (a ProjectDiscovery interactsh out-of-band callback domain), then issues `dns.resolve(...)` so the installer's recursive resolver leaks those host identifiers to the attacker-controlled authoritative nameserver. The package's `package.json` description self-identifies as a dependency-confusion proof-of-concept targeting the unclaimed `@aonunited` npm scope, and is published at version `99.0.0` — the standard version-bump trick used to outrank private-registry resolution. Any build system that resolves this scope from public npm will silently transmit its hostname and username to a third party at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
